dataSourceNote: The structured data pipeline (Yahoo Finance, Google News, SEC EDGAR) was unreachable again this session (policy-level 403s at the proxy). Web search was used for qualitative price/news context instead. No new BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER was sized off this data; the existing AAPL position was evaluated only against its own already-approved stop ($308) / take-profit ($338) plan, which has not been triggered. The reasoning field in data/decisions.json was written directly in Traditional Chinese per user request, to make the dashboard's decision explanations easier to read; this log keeps the underlying research notes in English for consistency with prior days' logs.

webSearchFindings.AAPL: Closed $333.74 on 2026-07-17 (+0.14%, momentum clearly slowing). Apple overtook NVIDIA as the world's most valuable company. HSBC upgraded to Buy from Hold; KeyBanc downgraded to Underweight with a $250 target (2026-07-14) vs Citi/Evercore ISI both reiterating $365 targets — a wide analyst split. RSI14 at 71.86 (overbought), 9.7% above its 20-day SMA ($303.63) and 21.6% above its 200-day SMA ($273.82). FQ3 earnings due 2026-07-30 (consensus EPS $1.89, revenue $108.86B). Now within ~1.5% of the position's original $338 take-profit. Source: benzinga.com, WebSearch aggregated finance pages.
webSearchFindings.MSFT: Climbed 1.8% to $402.70 on 2026-07-17, extending Wednesday's +2.8% (two-session +4.6%, adding ~$132B market cap) even as the Nasdaq fell ~1% in a chip-led selloff — a notable divergence. Citi cut its target to $570 (from $620, kept Buy); Morgan Stanley calls MSFT's generative-AI lead 'clear'. AI revenue run-rate exceeds $37B, growing 123%; Copilot paid seats over 20M. FY4Q26 earnings 2026-07-29. Source: foreignpolicyjournal.com (via WebSearch).

webSearchFindings.SPY/QQQ: S&P 500 (SPY) down -0.46%, Nasdaq 100 (QQQ) down -1.28% on 2026-07-17 (premarket had shown QQQ -1.86%, SPY -0.91%). Driven by a broad semiconductor selloff (Philadelphia Semiconductor Index down >13% over the trailing month, South Korea's Kospi -6% overnight on SK Hynix/Samsung weakness) compounding with the US military's sixth consecutive night of strikes on Iran. AAPL.status: Marked around $333.74 per web search (2026-07-17 close) — within ~1.5% of the original take-profit, RSI now overbought (71.86) with a notable bearish analyst outlier (KeyBanc Underweight, $250 target) emerging alongside the bulls. Neither stop nor target triggered; held unchanged per the original plan, flagged for close monitoring.

decisionProcess: Applied the system prompt v2's 7-role deliberation using the qualitative web-search findings above. 2026-07-17 was dominated by a broad semiconductor-sector rout (Philly Semi Index, Kospi, NVDA, META, AVGO all down) compounding a sixth consecutive night of US strikes on Iran — a systemic risk environment that blocks new position-taking regardless of individual setups. AAPL and MSFT diverged positively, but AAPL itself now shows an overbought RSI (71.86) and a sharp analyst-rating split (KeyBanc Underweight $250 vs Citi/Evercore $365) that argues for close monitoring rather than complacency, even though it sits close to its original take-profit. Every new-position candidate was vetoed at the Risk Manager step; the existing AAPL long continues unchanged since neither its stop nor its take-profit has triggered. Per user request, the reasoning field written into data/decisions.json for this and all prior decisions was translated into/written directly in Traditional Chinese to make the dashboard easier to read; this log's research notes remain in English for internal consistency.

entries._dataAsOf: 2026-07-17 (web search fallback — structured pipeline unreachable this session, see dataSourceNote)
